msContents.summary: [p]A collection of medical, cookery and veterinary receipt, etc. : mainly by two hands, one c. 1650, the other dated 1731-1739.[/p] [p]The original hand is that of ff. 1-82, of which ff. 1-38 are cookery receipts and the remainder medical. It also appears on 7 pp. at the end, containing veterinary receipts. At the end also are 11 pp. headed 'Dialectica est ars bene disserendi', partly in English and partly in Latin, by a different hand, also c. 1650.[/p] [p]Comprises the following recipes: Booke of receites howe to make a sweet cake For a potle of wine epecres Perfumed pufes; To preserve quinces To make marmelet; To preserve whit bulles or whit peare plomes To preserve barberys; To preserve aprecottes To preserve cheryes; To preserve goosbeery Losenges of roses or flowers; To preserve oranges and leamonds; Sirope of Fimetary Sirrope of vilolets; Sirope of clowgile flowers Sirope of rasberyes Sirope of orrenges ore Lemonds; To make mannes cristi; To make allmond Butter To make whit leach; To make allmond flurerdeles For a heghoge puden; Howe to make a lumber pye Sawse for a shoulder of mutton; Sause for a shoulder of moton To rost a shoulder of moton To make sturgon of a tarkey A stued lobster; Frid harty chocke A fremhe dishe A fremh dishe A fride rabett Sause of anchoves; Smaulde Sooles Frid chichens To peckelle oysters; For a boore; To make fine crackell cakes Mis killegrew cakes; To make gingerbread of allmonds; To make very fine Biscute; To make Hydromel To make mead; To make rosa solis or ros solis; To make treacle of herbes For a coold stomacke cowld An electuarie against malamolie; To stanche blood when the vaine is cutt Another of ##r Groopes Another To stanche bleden att the nose Another Another; For the ruketts A drinke for the same; Sirrup for the Rickets For the scurvie a excellent dreinke; A receipt for the scurvy Another For the rickets For convoltion fitts in a child Good for the nurce; To prevent convoltion fitts For convoltion fitts A electuary good for convoltion fitts; To make one to sleape For the rickets and consumtion; Medicine against the pectilence taught King Henry the Seventh King Henry the Eight sent this to the Lord Mayor of London against the plague, cont. on folio 52; cont. from folio 51, King Henry the eight sent this to the Lord Mayor of London against the plague For the plague in the guts in man; For a tetter To skine a sore For a canker in the mouth Another Another For a straine or wrince For a cutt or green wound; For the paulsies in the hands; For paine in the backe Another; For spitting of blood For any in the stomacke or infecttions truble For the ianndice Another for the yellow jaundice; To cause one to sleep For the stone collick For the worms; For paine in the teethe For a sweet breath A faire purgation; For the dropsce Another Good for the swelling in the body and leggs For the blood clenesing after the measels or sprenge off scabes, cont. on folio 60; cont. from folio 59, To bring out the smale pox or measells A great secret secret to cure a cough in 2 or 3 days A precious watter for the eyes; A perfect and a proved good water to take away the spotts of the smalepoxe; To make the face clean and fair and take awaye spottsFor the smale poxe and to bring them out; For the patient that have the small poxe Electuary against looseneffe Another for looseneffe and a could stomake For the smale pox or swine pocks to bring them out To cause one to sleep To cause one to sleepe; A very good broath to comfort stomack and nurithe the body; For the wind of mother and convoltions fitts For one thats trubled with winde and fleme; Most soueraine for wind; A very good water against Melamholy, cont. on folio 67; cont. from folio 66, A very good water against Melamholy; To still aqua vite To make the best aqua composite A very good aqua coposita to bee mixed with rosasolis cordiall and comfortable; Dotter Stenens water named aqua mirabila Surfi# water to take att any time and after meales if anythinge afend the stomack; Dockter Butlers cordiall; For one that have a feveresh distemper; Lucatellus oyntment or balsamum Admirable for wounds; The vertues of Lucatellus oyntment or balsamum; A receite to keep a bruse from gangren never failem; To make the splene plaster in May; Pills for the stomack The spleene pills Creame of tarter one ounce For the splene; A receipt for the grene sickenesse The electuarie for the greene sickenesse, cont. on folio 87; The Third day purge A powder for the winde; A receipt an excellent to cause the to bee loose and have constant stoles; Pills for the stomake; For a stufeing in the stomacke; A diett drinke to cleanse the Blood and stomack; For a Ruhms or Soreness in the Eyes To Pickel Cucumbers and French Beans; A Receipt to make chesecakes; To pickel French Beans; To Make Currant Wine To make a Burning Ointmet To Make Green Goosebury wines To make Cherry Marmalett; To Make Mead that Drinks like Sherry Another To Make Mead; A Receipt for the Dropsy and scurvey prescribed by Docts Brosen of Norwich To Pickel Walnuts Like Mangoes; A Receipt for Dressing of fish A Receipt to Make Mead, cont. on folio 103; A Receipt to Make Mead To Make ginger bread; Fricaoie of Rabits; To Make Elder Wine; To Make a Cake; For a Cough For a Cough; Cordiall Bitters To make a Rabbit pye; Cordiall bitters For a Consumption or Dropsye Of milk Drink for a Hecktick fever Me Shermans Purge; To Make Lemon Brandy To make alamode Beef; To Collar Eles To make a Sack-posset; To make Barly Cream To make Jellys To make an orange pudding; To make a Triggary of Chickens or Rabbittes To make Cows-lip-Wine; To make a Karrot-pudding To make Scotch Collops; To Stew a Carp To pott a Hare Mead that was made for ### Doro Styleman February 1731 A #e#t to cure the Biting of a mad-Dog; To make almond [illegible] by Dr Hoiomans order To make a brown Bread pudding; A Marrow Pudding A rectfet for pain in the stomack; A Full discovery of the Medicines given by Ms Joanna Stevens for Cure of the stone and gravel The powder is thus prepar'd; The pills ar thus prepared; The Method of giving these Medicines are as follows; An approved receipt for the Cure or prevention of the Murrain in Cattle To Make a cake Lemon Pudding Lemon Cream; To Make a Rice Pudding To Make a Bread Pudding To make Minie pyes; To make Water Vinegar To make spice cakes To pickle Mushrooms; To make Catchup To make red Marmalade of Quinces; A Recipe for making Hessian Soup; For a felon; Dialectica ese ars bine differsndi; Bene; Eodemg# Sirsu logica dicta est Inventio est prima pars Bialectica de invemendis argumentis &c # Inuentio est Pars Bialectica Prima De argumentis; Inrimendis Argumentu est quodad aliquid argumendu est affectum est etc Quod Affectum est Ad Arguendu Aliquid Ratio est in omnibus; Actus est in omnibusExistentia est in omnibusTime non existit nisi in veTime non potest separati a rebusQuales sant singula rationes sold st#se #sideratasinguld rationes affect## sunt ad argumenduGoldPer seArgumentu est artificiale and in artificiale; Artificiale prius in artificialeArgu artificiale estQuod ex se arguitArtif est primu vel a primo or#l#; cont. from folio 134, Artif est prima vel a primo or#l# Prima est Quod est sud originis Prima # artif originale ue# primogenea Primum es absolution aut comparatam Prima absoluta # Quod absolute a#gui#; A drinke for a sicke horse To prevent the murrain; For any straine or stiffeling of a horse For a horse that have Scaby heeles For the gravelling of a horse in the foot For the murrain in cows # and in and such catle To prevent the murrain For the murrain in pigs; For the could in the lungs or stomack of a horse For a horse that is broken winded For the farcy or by some called the farcion in a horse For a strain in the pastorn ioyut; For the plague in the guts of horses For the staggers and yellows For the scratches For the bots For bots and worms[/p]

msItem.title: Book of receites, A
msItem.filiation: Hashmarks (#) in the summary of recipes are used to indicate where the indexers were unable to read the original text.

objectDesc.supportDesc: 1 volume7 bl ll. + 87 ff. + 26 ll. + 97 bl. ll. + 5 bl. ll. 8vo. 16 x 11 cm. Original vellum gilt binding defective, both clasps wanting. A number of leaves have been cut out.

binding.p: 7 bl ll. + 87 ff. + 26 ll. + 97 bl. ll. + 5 bl. ll. 8vo. 16 x 11 cm. Original vellum gilt binding defective, both clasps wanting. A number of leaves have been cut out.

bibl.title: Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library
bibl.author: S. A. J. Moorat
bibl.publisher: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine

custodialHist.custEvent: Database description transcribed from S.A.J. Moorat, [i]Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library[/i] (London: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine, 1962-1973).

additional.surrogates: In addition, a microfilm copy is held as AMS/MF/232.

listBibl.bibl: Published (in microfilm form) in [i]Women in Medicine: Remedy Books, 1533 - 1865[/i], ed. Sara Pennell (Woodbridge, Connecticut: Primary Source Microfilm, 2004).

revisionDesc.change: Transformed Calm xml export to TEI xml file using a transformation scenario
Manual edits following automated Calm-TEI transformation
